The beer lineup rotates with the seasons, giving you an excuse to come back again... and again. Trust us, you’ll want to.
properties.name: Hop Garden Brewing (Belleville)

properties.address: 18 E Main St, Evansville, WI 53536, USA
properties.blurb: Next stop: Evansville. Same great beer, same small-batch approach, just with a little more barbecue smoke in the air. The taproom brings all the easygoing charm you’d expect from Hop Garden, and their BBQ menu takes things to the next level. Bring your appetite and your curiosity. Between the seasonal beers and the smoked eats, you’re in for a proper flavor adventure.
properties.name: Hop Garden Brewing (Evansville)

properties.blurb: Round out your Hop Garden tour with a trip to Paoli, where the Sugar River provides the soundtrack and the farm-to-pint vibes are strong. It’s the kind of place where time slows down, live music drifts through the air, and even your most Type-A friend forgets to check their email. Grab a beer, snag a picnic table, and let the good times do their thing. You’ve earned it.
properties.name: Hop Garden Brewing (Paoli)

properties.address: 3216 Judson St, Gig Harbor, WA 98335, USA
properties.name: Hop Pharm

properties.blurb: Home to Tampa’s first and only custom seltzer bar, we’re flipping the script on how seltzer is done. While most spots offer one or two taps, we hand you a crisp 4% ABV base and let you build your own flavor adventure — mix, match, and make it yours. Innovation drives everything we brew, from dessert-inspired Berliner Weisse to barrel-aged stouts layered with unexpected adjuncts. Bold, creative, and always a little surprising — our beers and seltzers are made to keep you coming back for the next pour.
properties.name: Hop State Brewing

properties.address: 22 E Birch Ave #2, Flagstaff, AZ 86001, USA
properties.blurb: At Hops on Birch, variety is the name of the game. With 31 rotating taps and up to 100 craft beers and beverages available in cans, you're guaranteed to find something new each time. It’s more than just a taproom though, with real dartboards, live music, and events keeping the atmosphere lively. Whether you're a die-hard beer enthusiast or just looking for a fun hangout spot, Hops on Birch has you covered.
properties.name: Hops On Birch

properties.blurb: HopScotch Café calls their beer “happy juice,” and to be honest, we can’t think of a better description. With 10 rotating drafts and 100 cans in the cooler, ordering your drink feels almost as high-stakes as deciding who you’re going to marry. The good news? It’s pretty damn hard to mess up. They also sell 6-packs that drinkers can customize themselves. It’s every DIYer’s dream.
properties.name: HopScotch Cafe

properties.address: 9201 Cockleshell Dr Unit 13, Bonita Springs, FL 34135
properties.blurb: At Hopsized Brewing Co, they've mastered the art of crafting high-quality brews with a touch of magic. They release newly curated beers every Thursday, making each visit an adventure for your taste buds From traditional lagers to Nebulous IPA and APAs, from stouts to Kölsch's and fruited sours, their diverse selection caters to every beer lover's palate. Step into their warm and inviting third space, and you'll find the perfect blend of art and science, where passion and precision come together.
properties.name: Hopsized Brewing Co

properties.address: 4925 W Bell Rd c9, Glendale, AZ 85308, USA
properties.blurb: At Hopsology, beer isn’t just a drink, it’s a study. With 21 taps dedicated to showcasing the best of Arizona’s craft scene (and a few guest stars from beyond), there’s always something new to discover with every pour. The lineup also includes wine, ciders, seltzers, and even non-alcoholic options, so everyone can join in on the fun. Think of it as a crash course in great taste, one glass at a time.
properties.name: Hopsology Taproom and Wine Bar
properties.deal: BUY ONE BEER GET ONE 1/2 OFF

properties.address: 100 Anastasia Blvd, St. Augustine, FL 32080, USA
properties.blurb: Hornski’s is your one-stop sip shop. Lagers, IPAs, cider, wine, seltzers and even kombucha. They’ve got a pour for every mood. Vinyl keeps the vibe spinning, the arcade keeps the scores rising, and food trucks keep the cravings in check. Come for the taps, stay for the tunes… and maybe beat your high score while you’re at it.
properties.name: Hornski's Brewing Co

properties.blurb: House Divided Brewery doesn’t just have playfully-named beers like Bat Out of Helles Bock, Tide is High Blonde Ale, and Drunken Sailor Irish Stout. It also offers canned seltzers, ciders, and bottles of wine for the “can we please go somewhere other than a brewery” drinkers. And despite its name, this Ely-based brewery is a house united when it comes to live music, cold beers, and their lounge-worthy patio.
properties.name: House Divided Brewery

properties.address: 1885 Elk River Rd, Steamboat Springs, CO 80487
properties.blurb: A love for fresh and natural ingredients meets a passion for crafting delicious pizzas come together at How Ya Doin Pizza n’ Eatz.! This quirky joint takes pride in serving top-notch food and creating a welcoming environment for all customers. With a strong focus on using natural and non-GMO ingredients, their extensive menu offers a delightful range of options. From delectable pizzas and mouthwatering sandwiches to refreshing salads and finger-licking appetizers, there's something to satisfy every taste bud, young or old. Whether you choose to sit at the lively bar or enjoy the laid-back patio setting, they'll find themselves surrounded by good vibes and great flavors. And don't forget to pair that perfect slice with a cold beer, available on tap, in bottles, or cans!
properties.name: How Ya Doin Pizza n' Eatz

properties.blurb: "We’re big fans of funky mountain town breweries, and this Rollinsville gem is one of the good ones. Perfect for a pit stop to or from Eldora Mountain Resort, swing by for jaw-dropping views, laid back vibes, a dog-friendly taproom, string band music on weekends—oh, and the beer ain’t nothing to sneeze at either. If you’re into Belgian-inspired offerings, you’re in luck—the affable couple behind Howlin Wind specialize in this sacred tradition. Start with a bright, fruity and eminently sessionable Dawn’s Early Light Saison before graduating to a splendidly dry-hopped Atop the Perch Farmhouse Ale. The more hop-forward crowd will enjoy an Everything’s Round Juicy Pale Ale, or Big Rock Park Hazy Terpene IPA with their Rocky Mountain High vistas. Kombucha is also on offer for those not into barley pops, and the brewery shares a space with a most excellent coffee shop. "
properties.name: Howlin Wind Brewing

properties.blurb: When we tell you Hubbleton Brewing Co. believes in handcrafted, we aren’t just talking about the beer. They also renovated the building, which they’ve had to expand twice as the brewery gained popularity. The current taproom has an indoor and outdoor beer garden, bar games, live music, and of course, great beer. Try the Crawfish River to see why it’s their #1 seller.
properties.name: Hubbleton Brewing Co

properties.address: 225 E Monroe St, Phoenix, AZ 85004, USA
properties.name: Huss Brewing (Monroe)

properties.blurb: From humble beginnings with a husband, wife, and an F-150 to becoming Arizona’s third-largest brewery, Huss has earned every bit of its reputation. Sure, you can grab a Papa’s Orange Blossom in just about any grocery store in the state, but the Uptown taproom is where the magic really happens. Between their lineup of crowd favorites and a food menu that’s basically begging you to order a giant pretzel with Cactus Valley Beer Cheese Fondue, this is the kind of place where “quick beer” turns into a full-on evening.
properties.name: Huss Brewing Downtown Brew Pub

properties.address: 1520 W Mineral Rd Suite 102, Tempe, AZ 85283
properties.blurb: This cozy taproom offers a wide selection of beers, from the Koffee Kolsch to the refreshing Orange Blossom, even sneaking in a few popular nationwide taps just to keep the locals on their toes. Huss Brewing Co. offers some of THE best beers in all of Arizona, so if you pop in while you’re roadtripping, be sure to snag a six pack or a case of your favorite to smuggle across the border when you head home. Just remember that beer is considered precious cargo in all fifty states.
properties.name: Huss Tempe Taproom

properties.blurb: Jeffery Paul Hyder decided to honor his great grandfather, Andrew Jackson Macgillvery, by opening a brewery. Macgillvery made the “best whisky in the lowest valley of the highlands,” and so Hyder carries on his legacy with a “wee taste of the highlands” at Hyderhead Brewing. Nestled in the heart of historic downtown DeLand, Hyderhead has a selection of Craft Scottish Brews, from the Bonnie B-Lass Smokie Pale Ale to the Maide Leise to the Weight Over the Bar, as well as a Scottish-inspired menu with Braunschweiger Liverwurst, Haggis and more.
properties.name: Hyderhead Brewing

properties.blurb: Cocktail menu: check. Pet-friendly: check. Beer: check, check, check. I.C. Brewhouse is everything you could want out of a brewery, with a full cocktail menu, pet-friendly patio, and 16 local beer taps. Honorable mentions: 17 sprawling TVs, a daily happy hour, and a signature brew: the Block Party Cranberry Wheat beer.
properties.name: I.C. Brewhouse (Centennial)

properties.address: 845 Serenoa Rd, Santa Rosa Beach, FL 32459
properties.blurb: Looking for a brewery that's always pushing the boundaries of their craft? Look no further than Idyll Hounds Brewing Co. With a commitment to sustainability, tradition, and a pioneering spirit, this brewery is always exploring new territory and rediscovering old favorites in the world of beer. And the proof is in the pint glass - try their Ghost Crab Pilsna' for a crisp and refreshing take on a classic style, or dive into the hazy goodness of their Boosh New England Style India Pale Ale. At Idyll Hounds, adventure, creativity, and great taste go hand in hand.
properties.name: Idyll Hounds Brewing Co

properties.blurb: A decade of glory in Minneapolis has paved the way for this remarkable 414 outpost. They're celebrating all that put them on the map in MN while venturing into the future with a pilot system that showcases their creative spirit. Not sure where to start? Fear not, for the Hello, Milwaukee brew is the perfect liquid welcome mat for thirsty PubPassers like us! Indeed has arrived in Milwaukee, and we're absolutely here for it. Raise your glasses to the exciting journey ahead you will not be disappointed.
properties.name: Indeed Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Three words for you: Pistacho. Cream. Ale. We brag about this beer everywhere we go. Lucky for you? It's a staple right here in Nordeast. Whether you belly up inside or grab a table near the tracks? You'll feel welcome at this ever-growing-but-always-original taproom. If you have friends in Cream City? Tell them Indeed's landed there now, too. If you don't know what Cream City is? That's okay. Just order another Cream Ale and smile, knowing you've got one of the best midwest breweries in your backyard.
properties.name: Indeed Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Beer and barbeque belong together. Nothing feels more natural than stepping into Industry for one of 65 local craft beers on tap (the largest selection in Naples) and tender pulled pork shoulder. All the meat is authentic and smoked daily, and if beef and barbeque weren’t enough, the walls are also covered with local art. Shop artists like Christopher Sweeney and Shelby Finger while you eat. Just make sure you don’t touch: we see that barbeque sauce on your fingers.
properties.name: Industry Beer & Barbeque

properties.blurb: A poker player, country singer, and tech bro walk into a bar. This could be the start of a really excellent joke, or just your average night at Inga’s. This quirky neighborhood pub has Bacardi and Bitcoin trivia, Monday night poker, Saturday night karaoke, and specialty cocktails to make it all go down smooth. And if you need a post-singing/poker/trivia snack, you’re in luck: the kitchen is open late.
properties.name: Inga's Alpine Tavern

properties.blurb: Already one of our favorite taprooms in MSP, Insight went and topped themselves by putting in a massive outdoor bar that's perfect for your next session. What are you drinking at that bar? We love Avant - a dry-hopped Pilsner that drinks like a dream. Or explore Insight's menu of 'House beers' - Light, Pale & Dark. If seasonals are more your jam? Keep an eye on their small batch system for ever-changing suds to complement your stay on a patio worth a return visit or two.
properties.name: Insight Brewing

properties.address: 2160 W Drake Rd A1, Fort Collins, CO 80526, USA
properties.blurb: At Intersect Brewing, crossing paths feels less like chance and more like a guarantee. You’ll be back again and again. Independently and family-owned, this is where craft beer, good food, and pinball all come together in one welcoming space. It’s the kind of intersection you don’t just pass through. You stick around for another round. ** Deal good on in house core beers **
properties.name: Intersect Brewing

properties.blurb: Iowa Distilling Co. honors the “Iowa” in their name. They buy corn from Iowan farmers. They were one of the first establishments in Iowa to get a native distilling license. And they’ve been voted the best local Iowan distillery by CityView for four years running. Speaking of awards, their Front of House manager has also been awarded Best Local Bartender – so expect some of that local Iowan charm when you stop in.
properties.name: Iowa Distilling Co

properties.blurb: Why visit just ONE brewery when you can visit ALL of Wisconsons best in one place? From a Ned Flanders by Titletown Brewing Co to an IceHenge Belgian-Style Dubbel from Tyraneana Brewing Co and everything in between. The best part? It’s self-serve so you can try as many brews as you want before committing. Come on the right night and you will be serenaded by some of the best local musicians. Come on any night and order the yummy pretzel bites. Thank us later.
properties.name: Iron Hop Taphouse

properties.address: 508 N Main St, Grain Valley, MO 64029
properties.blurb: Iron Kettle Brewing brings a touch of Europe’s old-world charm right to Grain Valley, Missouri with no passport or jet lag required. With a rotating lineup of guest taps and a solid selection of house favorites, there’s always a brew to keep your taste buds entertained. They partner with local farmers and suppliers to keep ingredients fresh and the community thriving. Every day, Iron Kettle serves up craft beer, friendly service, and good vibes in a space that feels like it was made just for hanging out.
properties.name: Iron Kettle Taphouse

properties.address: 514 Perry St c106, Castle Rock, CO 80104
properties.blurb: Iron Mule Brewing seamlessly combines old and new techniques to craft globally inspired, locally brewed beers. With a diverse lineup of  beers, from hop-forward ales to rich stouts, they deliver flavors that excite and satisfy. Experience the perfect blend of tradition and innovation at Iron Mule Brewing. Cheers to exceptional beer!
properties.name: Iron Mule Brewing

properties.blurb: Irwin Brewing Co doesn't mess around when it comes to brewing greatness. With their time-tested craft beer techniques and a touch of mountain wizardry, they've conjured up award-winning ales and lagers that'll leave you spellbound. From snowcat dreams to liquid reality, this brewery knows how to turn pristine snowmelt into pure liquid gold. Whether you're carving up the slopes or chilling with friends, a sip of their brews will have you dancing with delight. But it's not just about the beer—it's a celebration of adventure, camaraderie, and the wild spirit of Colorado's last great ski town.
properties.name: Irwin Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Picture this: a charming coastal byway leading straight to the doorstep of a remarkable brewery. Indeed, it's not just a whimsical notion; it's the reality offered by Islamorada Brewery & Distillery, beckoning all adventure-seekers. Enter Islamorada Brewery & Distillery, the embodiment of an exquisite blend of flavors. It's not just a brewery—it's also a distillery, offering an array of libations to satisfy every palate. With a draft list spanning a spectrum of beers and cocktails, from the timeless classics to novel small-batch creations, there's no shortage of options to tantalize taste buds.
properties.name: Islamorada Brewery & Distillery

properties.address: 2294 Mayport Rd, Atlantic Beach, FL 32233, USA
properties.blurb: Island Girl Taproom is where island time meets top-shelf pours. Craft beer, premium spirits, and a walk-in humidor give you plenty of ways to unwind. Whether you’re savoring a smoky cigar, sampling a rotating craft beer, or nursing a perfectly mixed cocktail, the laid-back vibe makes it easy to forget the clock. Think of it as your mini-vacation without the packing or the plane ride.
properties.name: Island Girl Mayport Taproom

properties.blurb: Let’s get one thing straight: you might come to an island to get away from it all, but “all” doesn’t include pizza. Voted the #1 pizza on Sanibel Island for twenty years running, Island Pizza Company clearly knows what they’re doing. Try a classic Hawaiian pizza, or feast on their weekly smoked BBQ special. Pair with one of the craft beers on tap for your own little slice of paradise.
properties.name: Island Pizza

properties.blurb: We love the simple things in life: fresh-cut grass, sunsets, and great beer made with the best hops, barley, and malts. The results? Top-notch craft brews that somehow manage to be both approachable and adventurous. Light and zesty Belgian Wits, playful sours, or layered, bold ales. You’ll find a lineup that’s anything but simple. Get ready for a seriously satisfying sip at Ivanhoe Park Brewing.
properties.name: Ivanhoe Park Brewing Co

properties.address: 23 N Orange Blossom Trl, Orlando, FL 32805, USA
properties.blurb: Didn’t have a chance to try that one beer calling your name last time? It’s your lucky day. The Lager House takes the best of Ivanhoe Park Brewing and pours it into a space made for lingering. Crisp, refreshing lagers and a rotating lineup of craft favorites make it easy to find your next obsession. Swing by, grab a pint, and finally give that elusive brew the attention it deserves.
properties.name: Ivanhoe Park Lager House

properties.blurb: Spaghetti and craft beer don’t typically go together. But at J Moe’s, it just works. This craft brewery operated for six years before introducing Italian cuisine, and the rest was… well, delicious. J Moe’s Italian Bistro & Brewery combines generational family recipes with a steadfast love of craft beer for a surprising — and delightful — combination.
properties.name: J Moe's Italian Bistro & Brewery

properties.blurb: Step up to the plate at J. Leinenkugel's Barrel Yard, where beer and baseball unite for an epic experience! Situated overlooking left field of American Family Field, open year-round, this brewery is a grand slam. Get ready to savor pilot brews crafted on site by the master brewers at Leinenkugel's while cheering on your favorite team. But it's not just about the hops and homers—treat your taste buds to mouthwatering Wisconsin comfort food with a twist of Northwoods flavor. Whether you're a beer enthusiast or a die-hard baseball fan, J. Leinenkugel's Barrel Yard is a winning destination that hits it out of the park every time! ** VALID ON NON-GAME DAYS ONLY*
properties.name: J. Leinenkugel's Barrel Yard

properties.blurb: Hold onto your hops, because Jackalope Brewing Co is here to unleash a wild world of delicious beer! These brewing rebels know how to brew with a sustainable twist, putting the "eco" in brewski. Sip on their legendary lineup of year-round brews, like the mighty Sarka Pilsner, an unruly concoction that'll make your taste buds dance with joy. But the fun doesn't stop there! Jackalope Brewing Co keeps things fresh with a rotating lineup of seasonal brews that will keep you coming back for more. Get ready to hop on the flavor train and embark on a beer-tastic adventure with Jackalope Brewing Co.
properties.name: Jackalope Brewing Co

properties.blurb: PSA to all Brewer’s fans: if you’re looking for a place to pregame, this is it. Jackson’s offers shuttle service for all home games! And if you’re not a Brewer’s fan, you can still come for the build-your-own-burger, Reuben rolls, rotating beer taps, seltzer, and wine. But let’s face it, if you’re a Brewer’s fan, this is the pre-game pub for you.
properties.name: Jackson's Blue Ribbon Pub

properties.blurb: JAK’s Brewing’s motto is “aggressive beer for aggressive beer drinkers.” That’s also a nod to the Air Force Aggressor program that helped inspire this brewery. Its logo is the red star, the symbol of the fallen Soviet Union that today is the unifying emblem of the Air Force Aggressor program. Aside from its Air Force ties, JAK’s Brewing also has a rotating food truck schedule from local options like Bite Me Gourmet Sausage and All Things Pita.
properties.name: JAKs Brewing (Colorado Springs)

properties.blurb: JAKs is a bold brewery whose beers aren’t for the faint of heart. Can you handle the hop? Try the JAKalope Double IPA if you’re ready to find out. If you’re a chocolate lover, take our advice and bet on the BlackJAK Porter. With food trucks, frequent events, and a dog-friendly taproom, even the shy ones will find something to love here.
properties.name: JAKs Brewing (Falcon)

properties.blurb: You go to Jams for dinner and open the menu. Then you do a double take, because there’s no way you just read the words “blue crab egg rolls,” “short rib grilled cheese,” and “lobster tacos.” Good news, friend: you aren’t dreaming. And guess what else? Jams has a huge patio, live music, and a full menu of beer. Yeah, we got emotional too. Here’s a tissue and a menu. Go live out your dream.
properties.name: Jams Old Market

properties.address: 2400 George Flagg Pkwy, Des Moines, IA 50321
properties.blurb: A flight of wine and a plate of cheese: ah, the simple things in life. Jasper Winery has been making their own wine for over 20 years. With plenty of seating indoors and out, it’s a great spot to bring friends or host an event. In summer, they put on a free outdoor concert series every Thursday: bring a chair or blanket and let them take care of the rest.
properties.name: Jasper Winery
properties.deal: 50% OFF FIRST TWO GLASSES OF WINE

properties.blurb: “JJ’s Place” sounds like the house of a good friend you’d hit up for an after-school, after-dinner, or after-work hang, and it’s a pretty fitting name. People head to JJ’s for live sports, for a bite to eat, for karaoke night, or for a poker game with their buddies. You can get 5 shots of Fireball for $20 at JJ’s, and $4 Root Beer Barrels. We know what you’re thinking. Typical JJ.
properties.name: JJ's Place

properties.blurb: The atmosphere at McGurk’s transports you straight to Dublin, with Irish musicians performing every night of the week, plus a generous selection of Ireland’s finest beers and whiskey. If drinking outdoors is your thing, the luck of the Irish has indeed found you. McGurk’s 15,000 square feet of space—complete with a waterfall and three outdoor bars—won Best Patio in St. Louis in 2021. Sláinte!
properties.name: John D. McGurk's

properties.address: 16905 Jowler Creek Rd, Platte City, MO 64079
properties.blurb: The wines here are as delightful as they are locally produced! Their award-winning creations are crafted with love and sold right here at the winery, available by the glass or bottle. If you get hungry they've got you covered with a mouthwatering assortment of gourmet cheeses, fresh-baked bread, locally-produced meats, and more. It's like a symphony of flavors, perfectly tuned to complement your wine tasting experience.The fun doesn't stop at the wines and food—Jowler Creek's tasting room is an experience in itself. Step into a world of charm and character, where the ambiance will whisk you away to a wine wonderland. And if you prefer the great outdoors, their beautifully-landscaped wine garden awaits you, nestled next to their sustainable vineyard.
properties.name: Jowler Creek Winery

properties.blurb: Gather around fellow mortals, for we have great gifts to bestow unto you from the majestic beer gods. Offering two core brews year-round - a delightful light lager or a citrus forward IPA - their rotating specialty beers are truly delectable. Add a plate of their New York Strip or a bowl of Beer Mac & Cheese for dinner and you’ll have a feast of ambrosia before you.
properties.name: Kalona Brewing Co

properties.blurb: We were sold on this place the second we heard about “Woofstock” — an annual dog-friendly music festival that benefits the Jacksonville Humane Society. (Seriously, how cool is that?) Northeast Florida’s first private indoor/outdoor dog park boasts 7,000 square feet of indoor, climate-controlled play area, plus an additional 7,000 square feet of outdoor space for your furry friend(s) to frolic about. There’s also a grooming service, doggy daycare, boarding options — even an on-site veterinarian! For human types needing a little hair of the dog, there’s a hip bar area with 15 local and regional craft beers on tap (also kombucha and nitro cold brew coffee), as well as “ruff-ly” 50 excellent options for canned beer and seltzers — “woof dah!”
properties.name: Kanine Social

properties.address: 310 W 79th St, Kansas City, MO 64114, USA
properties.blurb: At KC Bier Co, they’ve put the "I" back in "Bier," serving up German-style beers that are as authentic as they are refreshing. With malts sourced from a 160-year-old Bavarian maltster and imported German hops, these brews pack all the rich flavors of traditional German craftsmanship with a Kansas City twist. Lederhosen might not be required, but with bier this good, we wouldn’t be surprised if they catch on. YOUR PUBPASS IS VALID ON .5L BIERS ONLY
properties.name: Kansas City Bier Co

properties.blurb: The appeal of Keel Farms is two-fold: their winery and their agrarian ale & cider. Tour both any weekend for a trolley ride and behind-the-scenes experience. And if that trolley ride makes you wish there were a modern or traditional beer or glass of wine in your hand, all the better.
properties.name: Keel Farms

properties.address: 2501 Bienville Blvd, Ocean Springs, MS 39564, USA
properties.name: Keg & Barrel (Ocean Springs)

properties.blurb: The self-appointed “Valhalla of Nostalgia,” Jacksonville’s first ever arcade bar lives up to its lofty moniker by transporting you into a masterfully-curated, neon-filled shrine to 80s and 90s pop culture. Classic video games abound, from Rampage to NBA Jam to Mortal Kombat. Foosball, skee-ball, a choice selection of ever-rotating pinball machines — it’s all here. They even sell packs of Garbage Pail Kids trading cards and play retro cartoons on Saturday mornings! Did we mention beer yet? Choose from 15 rotating taps and a staggering selection of bottles and cans that span the entire country’s finest purveyors of craft suds. Check their website and socials for special events galore.
properties.name: Keg & Coin

properties.blurb: We’d expect nothing less than a warm, kind, friendly atmosphere from an Iowa brewery. Lots of places in Iowa are just a little bit out of the way, but if you’re willing to drive just a bit, you won’t regret it. Be careful, though, you might think you're swinging by just for the staple Midwest Haze but then you find yourself accidentally joining a cornhole league with your buddies. Don’t say we didn’t warn you.
properties.name: Keg Creek Brewing

properties.address: 355 Iris Avenue, Jefferson, LA 70121 Suite A
properties.blurb: Ever held an apple in your hand and marveled at the ability of people much smarter than you to turn it into hard cider? Get ready to start feeling the same way about pears, my friend... Kingfish makes hard cider and Perry... a fun way to pear-derived alcohol. We know, it's new and you've always been slow to adopt trends (we weren't going to mention those shoes). That's ok. Grab a Prohibition (dry Perry) and you'll start to understand.
properties.name: Kingfish Cider

properties.blurb: Kinkaider’s newest taproom in Omaha has the vibe of a neighborhood bar crossed with a cozy cocktail lounge. That’s pretty appropriate though, because in addition to their many beers, they also serve craft cocktails and cider courtesy of their side hustle, Sideshow Spirits. Regular bingo and trivia nights make this a must-visit spot.
properties.name: Kinkaider Brewing

properties.blurb: As a locally owned and operated micro-brewery deeply rooted in Rochester, they've mastered the art of crafting lip-smacking beers and tantalizing hard seltzers. The Taproom is a haven of good vibes, where folks gather to savor the flavors and share laughter. Kinney Creek Brewery proudly presents a lineup of craft brews and hard seltzers that'll make taste buds dance the tango of delight! And oh, don't miss their claim to fame – the "Med City Seltzer" – boasting an impressive 25 flavors on tap that'll take your seltzer game to soaring heights! With each visit to Kinney Creek Brewery, you're in for a tail-wagging experience. Their fun-loving atmosphere and creative libations are a recipe for endless smiles. So gather your crew, unleash your adventurous spirit, and let the good times flow at Kinney Creek Brewery!
properties.name: Kinney Creek Brewery

properties.blurb: Step into beer heaven at Kippy's Place, where the taps flow with liquid gold and the vibes are always electric. This vibrant joint is a mecca for beer lovers, offering an unrivaled selection of brews that will make your taste buds sing. Kippy's Place has the perfect pint to quench your thirst and ignite your senses. And let's not forget about the grub! Their menu is a gastronomic adventure, with irresistible pub fare that will make your mouth water. So gather your crew, raise your glasses, and get ready for a legendary experience at Kippy's Place! Cheers to good times and even better beer!
properties.name: Kippy's Place

properties.blurb: The folks at Knotted Root Brewing look at brewing beer the same way they look at life, a work in progress, constant evolution and crossing a bridge into the unknown. They push the boundaries with their brewing process. Can you say hoppy Gummy Bears? Where can we sign up? While they focus on those amazing hoppy beers they also love to experiment with those rustic, mixed-culture farmhouse ales so there is something for everyone. With their ever changing line up of brews we never know what we are in for, but we know it’s going to be amazing.
properties.name: Knotted Root Brewing

properties.address: 842 Market St, San Diego, CA 92101, USA
properties.blurb: Deal valid on Knotty Barrel beers
properties.name: Knotty Barrel

properties.address: 13782 E I25 Frontage Rd Unit B1, Longmont, CO 80504
properties.blurb: Grab your buddies and get in formation* at Knuckle Puck Brewing. These folks know their beer like Gordon Bombay knows a triple deke. The tap list is stacked with something for everyone, from hoppy IPAs to smooth stouts, plus a few fun surprises along the way. Ducks fly together to happy hour at this cozy taproom. *Flying V highly encouraged, but all formations are welcome.
properties.name: Knuckle Puck Brewing

properties.blurb: Look no farther for your go-to tavern, this place is the only correct answer. With their killer steak dinner deal on Monday, the double stack of trivia AND poker night on Thursday, and karaoke on Sunday you’d just be a silly goose to go anywhere else. The menu offers classic tavern eats and drinks and the ambience offers classic settle in on your favorite barstool and get ready to chill vibes. Sundays are our favorites, so come enjoy us butcher our most favorite Celine Dìon songs.
properties.name: Knucklehead Tavern

properties.blurb: Nothing splashy here, just folks who take the freshest, highest quality ingredients and turn them into delicious craft beer and serve artisan pizzas from a wood-fired oven built with their own two hands. Okay, so maybe it’s a little splashy. Pair one of those pizzas with an Alexander Pilsner or the Classic Lager. Are we bummed we weren’t in town for the mac and cheese pizza of the week? Yes, yes we were. A little heads up next time, friends.
properties.name: Knuth Brewing Co

properties.address: 3027 E Harmony Rd Suite #2, Fort Collins, CO 80528, USA
properties.name: Konstruct Brewing

properties.address: 9030 Kenamar Dr #309, San Diego, CA 92121, USA
properties.name: Kove Brewing

properties.blurb: Act like a local: pronounce it coyote. You’ll fit right in. The indoor sand volleyball court is a surefire standout and know that your killer aces will serve as the backdrop for the live bands Kyote’s brings in frequently. Friendly barkeeps, flatscreens for the game, and even a couple of pinball machines wrapped around a sandwich and wing-centric menu make it a local favorite of Packer Country.
properties.name: Kyote's Bar & Grill

properties.blurb: Are you the kind of person that looks at a menu days in advance so you have time to strategize a plan of attack? No? That’s just us? Seriously, everything on the menu at Labelle is mouthwatering, from the shrimp and grits to the pretzel with beer cheese. Might we suggest starting up a text thread with your pals to see what they are interested in ordering and go from there. Be sure to include friends who know how to share, you’re going to want a bite of everything.
properties.name: Labelle Brewing Co

properties.address: 14141 Airline Hwy bldg 3 suite a, Baton Rouge, LA 70817, USA
properties.blurb: Laissez Versez Distillery is bringing Bourbon back to its roots, with a distinctly Louisiana twist. Committed to using local ingredients and pushing the boundaries of traditional aging, they’ve crafted a Bourbon rich in both history and flavor. Expect strong notes of oak and vanilla, a smooth hint of caramel, and a finish that lingers just right. Proudly rooted in the Bayou State, Laissez Versez is for those who appreciate quality, tradition, and a good time. So, when it’s time to raise a glass, let the good times pour with Laissez Versez Bourbon!
properties.name: Laissez Versez Distillery

properties.blurb: In the charming abode of Lake 11 Brewing, one discovers a harmonious blend of chill lake vibes and unbridled merriment, all woven together into a delightful brew-tastic experience! This brewery draws inspiration from the surrounding nature and the abundant recreational opportunities in the region. With utmost dedication, Lake 11 Brewing has masterfully crafted a collection of beers that guarantees a celebration of life's simple pleasures. The inviting ambiance encourages visitors to let go, savor each moment, and indulge in the flavors of their delightful concoctions.
properties.name: Lake 11 Brewing

properties.blurb: Imagine warm sunshine, a rope swing inviting you, and a crisp, refreshing brew in hand. That's the kind of experience you'll find at Lake Louie. Their beers are easy-drinking and crafted for those who know how to have a blast. It doesn’t matter if you choose an IPA, a pilsner, or a pale ale, you can bet it's going to be top-notch. They only use the finest ingredients, and the result? Well, it's a whole lot of flavor, an easy-drinking experience, and pure enjoyment in every sip.
properties.name: Lake Louie Brewing

properties.blurb: At Lakefront, we recommend the Bumble Bear, a rich tasting Honey Brown Ale with ample fresh-baked biscuits, clover honey and notes of chocolatey, roasted malt. We also recommend the 45-minute brewery tour with optional adds of local cheese, meat, fruit, chocolate, and coffee. Oh, and we definitely recommend the Date Night in Bernie’s Chalet, a romantic dinner overlooking the fermentation cellar. Wait, are you writing this all down?
properties.name: Lakefront Brewery

properties.blurb: LandLocked Ales may not be on the ocean, but that doesn’t mean they don’t know their liquids. Sip everything from an IPA to a classic light beer to a stout at this brewery, or ask for a kombucha instead. Need something to nosh on while you’re there? They also offer locally-made kettle corn, potato chips, and ice cream.
properties.name: LandLocked Ales

properties.address: 5693 New Natchitoches Rd, West Monroe, LA 71292, USA
properties.blurb: The BOGO Tasting at Landry Vineyards will include one tasting board of 6 -1 oz. tastings of your choice of 28 wines along with a Free Landry Vineyards wine glass to take home.  The second tasting is one tasting board of 6 -1 oz. tasting of your choice of 28 wine.  A wine glass to take home is NOT included in the BOGO, just the wine tasting.
properties.name: Landry Vineyards LLC

properties.blurb: Get ready to saddle up and experience the delightful charm of Lariat Lodge! This hidden gem offers a perfect blend of classic brewing techniques and upscale comfort food. Whether you're in the mood for a pint of their expertly crafted beer or craving some mouthwatering dishes, Lariat Lodge has got you covered. With an abundance of outdoor seating, including a dog-friendly area, you can enjoy your meal while soaking in the stunning mountain views.
properties.name: Lariat Lodge Brewing

properties.blurb: The word “lodge” practically oozes cozy and Lariat Lodge Brewing Co. doesn’t disappoint. Think juicy burgers, craft beers brewed just right, and comfort food that’s fancy enough to make your taste buds dance. There’s warm vibes inside, plenty of outdoor seats, and a dog-friendly policy that means your four-legged buddy won’t be stuck outside pining for you. Come for the food and brews, stay because you forgot to leave. We won’t judge.
properties.name: Lariat Lodge Brewing Co

properties.address: 4170 Morena Blvd suite f, San Diego, CA 92117, USA
properties.name: Latchkey Brewing Company

properties.blurb: Just a click away from the bustle of Fort Laudy sits LauderAle. A gem of a taproom with a sprawling beer garden where you can enjoy their suds with your dog. Or your friends. Or your friends’ dogs. Whatever works. What are you drinking? You’re starting with a light beer from their list, trust us. Smooth and crushable. From there? Get crazy and try one of their aged dark beers… Truly, they have it all.
properties.name: LauderAle Brewery

properties.blurb: Get ready for a hop-tastic journey at Launch Pad Brewery where craft beer is their rocket fuel. Crafted with top-notch ingredients since 2014. It all started with a pumpkin-themed party where a pumpkin beer stole the show, and the next day, David began working on his brewery dream. Keep an eye on their schedule as they always have something fun happening, such as trucks like Angry Chicken, a German food truck, Colorado Coney Company, and even thrilling trivia nights. Join them for a stellar experience at Launch Pad Brewery, where good times and great brews collide. Cheers to that!
properties.name: Launch Pad Brewery

properties.address: 2326 N Flint Ave, Portland, OR 97227, USA
properties.name: Lazy Days Brewing Co

properties.address: 4505 SW Watson Ave, Beaverton, OR 97005, USA
properties.name: Lazy Days Brewing Co (Beaverton)

properties.blurb: Get ready to experience the laid-back charm and award-winning flavors of Lazy Magnolia, Mississippi's oldest packaging brewery. Since 2005, they've been crafting brews that capture the essence of Southern hospitality and delight beer enthusiasts far and wide. Step into "The Porch," their cozy tap room that serves as a gathering spot for Lazy fans, both new and old. Sip on their acclaimed beers, savor delicious food, and soak in the scenic views that surround you. Whether you're a seasoned beer aficionado or just dipping your toes into the world of craft brews, Lazy Magnolia offers an unforgettable experience that combines great beer, tasty bites, and a warm Southern welcome. Cheers to lazy days, good vibes, and memorable moments at Lazy Magnolia!
properties.name: Lazy Magnolia

properties.address: 101 S Hummell St, Denham Springs, LA 70726
properties.blurb: Le Chien is all about handcrafted beers made with premium ingredients and a meticulous attention to detail. But their passion doesn't stop at brewing exceptional beers. They also are dedicated to bringing quality music acts, delectable food, and exciting events to the community. So, whether you're looking for a refreshing brew, live entertainment, or a lively gathering spot, Le Chien Brewing Co. has you covered. Embrace the vibrant atmosphere, indulge in their remarkable beers, and immerse yourself in the spirit of community at this fantastic brewery. Cheers to great beer and good times!
properties.name: Le Chien Brewing Co

properties.address: 1760 Wells Rd, Orange Park, FL 32073, USA
properties.blurb: Get ready to unleash your appetite – they've got some seriously awesome gamer-inspired bites and craft beers to keep you fueled up between rounds. The best part? Leaderboard Arcade and Pizzeria is like stepping into a gamer's paradise. They've got a game collection that'll blow your mind. From the OG retro classics that everyone loves to the freshest releases that'll make your heart race, it's a gaming haven. Trust us, you'll walk in hungry and walk out a high-scoring legend.
properties.name: LeaderBoard Arcade Bar & Pizzeria

properties.address: 5285 Shad Rd Suite 316, Jacksonville, FL 32257, USA
properties.name: LeaderBoard Arcade Bar & Pizzeria (Jacksonville)

properties.blurb: You might know Left Hand Brewing for their Milk Stout Nitro, the beer that put them on the map as leaders of the nitro revolution. Worried they’ve been resting on their laurels? Think again. Their taproom is always full of surprises, from barrel-aged beers to delights like the Wook Bait IPA. If it’s more nitro you’re after, don’t sleep on the Flamingo Dreams, a fan favorite.
properties.name: Left Hand Brewing Co

properties.blurb: If you're looking for a spot to grab a drink and some delicious food before or after a concert at the Mission Ballroom, look no further than Left Hand RiNo Drinks and Eats! With 40 taps of beer, wine, and cocktails, there's something for everyone to enjoy while you soak up the vibrant atmosphere of the River North Art District. And if you're hungry, their eclectic menu has got you covered. From Neapolitan-style pizzas fresh out of their custom Marra Forni brick oven to shareable plates like their famous house made queso with tortilla chips, you won't go hungry here. So whether you're in the mood for a quick snack or a full meal, Left Hand RiNo Drinks and Eats is the perfect spot.
properties.name: Left Hand RiNo Drinks & Eats

properties.blurb: Legends of Aurora calls themselves a sports bar—and it’s true they’re chock-full of TVs, beers, and good eats. But oh, my friend, they are so much more. Want to eat fish tacos and perform your poem at an open mic? Come on by. More into UFC fight night, paired with wings? We’ll save a seat at the bar for you. Can’t decide between bingo and karaoke? At Legends, you never have to choose.
properties.name: Legends Of Aurora

properties.address: 1391 S Military Trail, Deerfield Beach, FL 33442
properties.blurb: At Legends Tavern, every night is a legendary experience with their incredible weeknight specials! Mondays are all about savoring mouthwatering burgers, while Wednesdays are dedicated to the smooth allure of Whiskey. And on Thursdays, it's time to pair delightful brews with scrumptious dogs!  But that's just the beginning of the tantalizing adventure awaiting you at Legends Tavern. Their menu is packed with bar favorites that will make your taste buds sing with joy. From delectable entrees to delightful bites, they have something to satisfy every craving.
properties.name: Legends Tavern (Deerfield)

properties.blurb: Legends is more than just a sports bar. Sort of like how you’re more than just a craft beer drinker. You also love signature cocktails (you’re in luck here), Smoked Fish Dip (weird flex, bud, but you’re also in luck here!) and nachos so big they make them on a sheet pan (once again, this is your spot). So use this page at Legends in Deerfield, Lighthouse Point, or Palm Beach Gardens and show the world (or at least your buddies) just how well-rounded you are!
properties.name: Legends Tavern (Plantation)

properties.blurb: The Legends legend continues when you redeem this page at Pompano Beach, Sunrise or Palm Beach Gardens. If you hit the menu hard at your first Legends stop? Get ready to strap on the feedbag once again. If you didn’t? Bro. What part of ‘Creamy Brie & Bacon Burger’ did you not understand? Do we need to bring your mom down so she can convince you to eat something already?
properties.name: Legends Tavern (Pompano)

properties.address: 10079 W Oakland Park Blvd, Sunrise, FL 33351
properties.blurb: At Legends Tavern, every night is a legendary experience with their incredible weeknight specials! Mondays are all about savoring mouthwatering burgers, while Wednesdays are dedicated to the smooth allure of Whiskey. And on Thursdays, it's time to pair delightful brews with scrumptious dogs!  But that's just the beginning of the tantalizing adventure awaiting you at Legends Tavern. Their menu is packed with bar favorites that will make your taste buds sing with joy. From delectable entrees to delightful bites, they have something to satisfy every craving.
properties.name: Legends Tavern (Sunrise)

properties.blurb: Leinie Lodge & Beer Garden captures the exuberant essence of Wisconsin's pride, authenticity, and camaraderie, all while situated in the heart of Baltimore. Rooted in a heritage spanning six generations, this establishment's beer and dining experience proudly display the finest ingredients and a warm hospitality. Nestled at the entrance of Power Plant Live!, Leinie Lodge & Beer Garden boasts a captivating glass pavilion adorned with a retractable roof and glass garage doors, creating a seamless connection to the inviting beer garden embellished with lawn games and an outdoor fireplace. With an offering of more than 30 specialty draft brews, this establishment has evolved into a downtown Baltimore hotspot. Leinie Lodge & Beer Garden stands as the quintessential destination for celebrations, social interactions, or simply unwinding with exceptional fare and remarkable beer. So, why not join us out here?
properties.name: Leinie Lodge & Beer Garden

properties.blurb: Believe us when we say we’ve visited a lot of bars over the years, and we’ve never seen a place quite like Leopold’s. Bottles of wine displayed next to books? A full bar right beside full bookshelves? It shouldn’t work, but somehow it does. Open til midnight six days a week so you can start off drinking cappuccino while reading a novel, then order an espresso martini when you aren’t ready for the night to end.
properties.name: Leopold's Books Bar Cafe

properties.blurb: Some people keep their head down, work hard, and produce something magical. That’s exactly what’s happened at Levi Garrison & Sons. Dedicated to brewing excellent craft beers, this microbrewery is a hidden gem located in a historic telephone company building. With twelve beers on tap, cozy seating, and delivery options from local restaurants, it’s about to be your favorite neighborhood bar.
properties.name: Levi Garrison & Sons

properties.address: 7539 Wornall Rd, Kansas City, MO 64114, USA
properties.blurb: Welcome to Lew's Grill and Bar, where Das Boot reigns and the good times flow. With plenty of TVs for all your game-day needs, cozy indoor and outdoor dining, and heated, covered patios, it's got that "neighborhood gem" vibe. Even if you're not from around here. You might be tempted to move closer just to make it your local spot, but hey, moving's a hassle. Why bother when you can swing by anytime and enjoy a cold one, no matter where you're from?
properties.name: Lew's Grill & Bar

properties.address: 1900 Tower Dr W, Stillwater, MN 55082, USA
properties.name: Lift Bridge Brewing Stillwater

properties.address: 8900 W Greenfield Ave, West Allis, WI 53214
properties.blurb: How many chicken wing sauces is too many? At Limanski’s, the limit doesn’t exist. The owner and namesake of this local favorite created 30 tasty, zingy sauces herself to keep her menu fresh and sizzling. Plus, on Mondays and Wednesdays, you can score 50 cent wings and $7 domestic pitchers — and on Fridays there is an all-you-can-eat Fish Fry. Comfort food never tasted better.
properties.name: Limanski's Pub

properties.blurb: You weren’t about to underestimate Iowa, were you? Nah, we know you wouldn’t do a thing like that. Especially not with a local spot like Limestone Brewers, which by the way, is located “in the middle of everywhere.” Besides incredible beers and pizza, you can also try your hand at axe throwing when you stop by the taproom. So, you were saying?
properties.name: Limestone Brewers

properties.address: 301 SE Douglas St, Lee's Summit, MO 64063
properties.blurb: You might just forget you’re not in Ireland at Llywellyn’s Lees Summit, what with the traditional Irish fare, Guinness mural, and regular live music. But the 40 beers on tap will remind you: Ireland could never. Make sure to soak up the beautiful patio when the weather’s warm (and hunker down inside for a pint when it isn’t!)
properties.name: Llywelyn's Pub

properties.address: 320 S Weber St, Colorado Springs, CO 80903
properties.blurb: If you worship at the altar of beer the way we do, you’re gonna love Local Relic. Housed in a historic church turned food hall, they aren’t tied down by flagships, instead tapping 200 different beers a year. Pair your beer with food offerings covering everything from small plates to all-day brunch. Since the brews change so often, be sure to take home a few bottles of your favorites before they’re gone!
properties.name: Local Relic Artisan Ales

properties.address: 5950 S Platte Canyon Rd, Littleton, CO 80123
properties.blurb: The dictionary definition of locavore is “a person who eats locally grown foods whenever possible,” but let’s all agree to add drinking to that too. Locavore Beer Works is ready to welcome you to that lifestyle, with an impressive rotation of craft beers and a dog-friendly, family-friendly taproom to drink them in. From the local honey in their Beezy Does It to the spices in the Sugar Magnolia Belgian Wit, local sure tastes good.
properties.name: Locavore Beer Works

properties.blurb: Here’s a nugget for you all trivia fans: 60% of the USA’s apples come from Washington. No surprise then that powerhouse Locust Cider planted their roots in WA and have been branching out (see what we did there?) ever since. This new kid on the block knows their stuff. Try the Vanilla Bean Cider—reminiscent of cream soda and real vanilla beans. Feel guilty cheating on Colorado cideries? Locust uses as many local ingredients as possible. So go take that guilt of yours and buy the first round.
properties.name: Locust Cider

properties.address: 7260 W Alaska Dr Unit A, Lakewood, CO 80226
properties.blurb: Looking for a spot to enjoy a refreshing and diverse selection of ciders? Look no further than Locust Cider in Belmar, Colorado! With an expansive cider menu, there's something for every taste bud. From their Juicy Peach cider to their Mojito cider and Blackberry cider, Locust has crafted an impressive lineup of unique and delicious ciders. But it's not just the cider that makes Locust a standout spot. They also offer indoor seating, a large patio perfect for sipping in the sun, and retro video games to keep you entertained while you sip. Need we say more? We’ll see you there.
properties.name: Locust Cider Belmar

properties.blurb: Welcome to Lombardy’s Tavern on 7th Street — where Wisconsin tavern flavor meets Redmond hospitality. Butter burgers, golden cheese curds, crispy wings, and cold beer set the stage for game days, family dinners, and nights out with friends. Pull up a chair, grab a pint, and stay awhile.
properties.name: Lombardy's Tavern

properties.blurb: All aboard the London Underground, where the brews are as bold as Big Ben and as diverse as the bustling city streets! This hoppy haven boasts an impressive lineup of beers, featuring a mix of local brews and international delights that will have you raising your pint in cheer. From smooth stouts to hoppy ales, each sip will transport you to a world of flavors and brewing traditions. So, hop on the beer train, join the hoppy hour crew, and experience the finest brews that London and beyond have to offer.
properties.name: London Underground

properties.address: 5000 Olney Laytonsville Rd, Olney, MD 20832, USA
properties.blurb: Great brews, historic views, thats what you'll get at Lone Oak Brewing. Here the beer is fresh, and the vibes are even fresher. It’s not just a brewery on a farm, it’s a farm with a brewery. They grow a lot of what goes into their beers, so you know it’s farm-to-glass goodness. Bold flavors, delicious farm bites (think pizza and handhelds), and a laid-back atmosphere that’ll make you want to stay a while. It’s basically a history lesson with a side of beer and pizza. You didn’t know you needed that combo, did you?
properties.name: Lone Oak Farm Brewing Co

properties.blurb: With 12 constantly changing beers on tap, there's always something new and exciting to try. But if that's not enough, their self-serve open coolers offer a staggering selection of over 300 brews for you to explore. And let's not forget about the food! Indulge in one of their expertly crafted charcuterie board or explore their full menu of mouthwatering bites. Whether you choose to enjoy your beer at a communal table or take it to-go, Longtable Beer Cafe is the perfect spot to unwind and discover the flavors that tickle your taste buds. So come on in and make yourself at home in this downtown Middleton treasure.
properties.name: Longtable Beer Cafe

properties.blurb: This Denver restaurant doesn’t claim to be authentic. But it does claim to be delicious. Los Chingones was inspired by Chef Troy Guard’s teen years in the coastal metropolis of San Diego. Every month it partners with a local celebrity chef to bring their own unique spin on the classic taco. Los Chingones also offers a rotating selection of canned beer, margaritas (claro que si), and irreverent, spicy décor.   "
properties.name: Los Chingones

properties.blurb: We have five words for you: monthly rotating celebrity chef taco. This Central Park location also boasts $5 happy hour with tostada pizza and borracho street corn, a niños menu for the little ones, and delicious desserts like the refreshing Chupacabra popsicle. Claro, they also have a margarita and beer menu.
properties.name: Los Chingones (Central Park)

properties.address: 3541 E Harmony Rd #110, Fort Collins, CO 80528
properties.blurb: We have five words for you: monthly rotating celebrity chef taco. This Fort Collins location also boasts $5 happy hour with Mexican elotes and daily quesadillas, a niños menu for the little ones, and delicious desserts like churros with Mexican chocolate and housemade mole cookies. Claro, they also have a margarita and beer menu.
properties.name: Los Chingones (Fort Collins)

properties.blurb: Just when you think the classic combo of tacos and margs can’t get any better, along comes Los Chingones. Their DTC location in Belleview Station has a great patio and giant windows that let you soak up the sun like you’re on an actual Mexican vacay. A $5 happy hour menu on weekdays gets the party started while the full menu includes smothered burritos, pork belly tacos, and more delicious options.
properties.name: Los Chingones DTC

properties.address: 63 E 2nd St A, Mineola, NY 11501, USA
properties.name: Lost Farmer Brewing Co.

properties.address: 213 4th St E #100, St Paul, MN 55101, USA
properties.name: Lost Fox

properties.blurb: It gets hot along the Gulf Coast. Experts say that hydration is key. Our friends at Lost Spring Brewing Co. conveniently have libations to help with this. They’ve taken the time to develop a small batch lineup to impress even the pickiest drinkers. The bartenders have perfected the art of crafting unique cocktails for all our thirsty friends along the coast. But don’t take our word for it – order a Sunken Sun, settle in, and let the live music wash over you like the waves on the shore.
properties.name: Lost Spring Brewing Co

properties.address: 9420 S Shrout Rd, Grain Valley, MO 64029, USA
properties.blurb: Lotawana Brewing Co. set sail with a clear mission to brew traditional ales and lagers that hit all the right notes. The result? A flagship lineup that’s simple, flavorful, and rooted in craft. Add in quick bites from the Getting Baked kitchen, and you’ve got the perfect pairing for any pint. And don’t sleep on Sunday brunch. It’s the kind of meal that makes the weekend feel complete.
properties.name: Lotawana Brewing Co

properties.blurb: This family-owned brewery is a staple in the heart of downtown Loveland, and its quaintness is definitely part of the charm. While it has a smaller beer lineup than some some regional neighbors, Loveland Aleworks offers a little bit of everything, and it’s clear their focus is on quality over quantity. The friendly atmosphere, patio seating, and a commitment to community are the best reasons to swing by this brewery when you find yourself in Loveland — or a great excuse for a day trip!
properties.name: Loveland Aleworks

properties.blurb: Loveland Tap and Tavern is your go-to spot for the best Colorado has to offer in beers, wines, and spirits. Their cozy lounge setting is perfect for sampling the finest local beverages, including a whiskey selection that boasts over 300 choices—paradise for whiskey lovers. With a food truck serving up mouthwatering burgers and sandwiches, you won't leave hungry. Plus, they’ve got treats for your furry friends, making it a perfect hangout for you and your pup.
properties.name: Loveland Tap & Tavern

properties.blurb: "Lowbrow believes in a lot of things. Like that pizza should be charred, crispy, and chewy. That beer and pizza make the perfect pairing. And that yes, pineapple does taste good with tomato sauce and layers of hot cheese. Come in for a local beer on tap, like Babycakes Stout or Hammock Hero Citrus Ale. Stay for a Pepper Bro-ni pizza or a Dad Bod salad. Or, our personal favorite, the Fusterclucks wings. "
properties.name: LowBrow Pizza & Beer

properties.blurb: With 9,000 square feet of outdoor space, picnic tables that can hold up to 500 people and a huge selection of both draft and bottled beers we can only wish our home garden was as cool as Lowry Beer Garden. Not only all of that but they also have a grill with casual fare with locally-made gourmet brats and sausages, freshly baked pretzels and house ground burgers. Bring your whole family for a backyard party with friends you just haven’t met, yet.
properties.name: Lowry Beer Garden

properties.blurb: At Luce Line Brewing Co, the only thing longer than the Luce LIneTrail is the list of beers you'll want to try. Their award-winning lineup of brews and seltzers keeps things classic. while rotating seasonal pours keep you guessing in the best way. Hungry? With some of the best food trucks around and a line-up of their own food, you'll never leave on an empty stomach. And a spacious, pet-friendly patio with fire pits for chilly nights, and it's the perfect place to kick back and stay awhile- whether you biked in or just came thirsty.
properties.name: Luce Line Brewing

properties.blurb: Sure, lots of breweries have pizza on the menu. But Lucette Brewing is raising the bar, firing up their brick oven to serve fresh, Neapolitan-style pizza right on site. The charming building that houses the brewery reminds us of someone’s grandparent’s farmhouse, with outdoor seating and a spacious, two-story interior. For a well-balanced meal, pair your pizza with any of their tasty beers—we recommend I Love It When You Call Me Big Hoppa.
properties.name: Lucette Brewing Co

properties.blurb: You’re gonna satisfy that taco hankering sooner or later, so lace up those calf-high wrestling boots and stroll over Luchador. Six taps including the always reliable Moon Man Pale Ale from New Glarus. The tequila list read like an encyclopedia with upwards of two dozen options on our last swing through. House margaritas help soften the bite of any of those tequila options, and a few outdoor tables on the patio allowed us to soak the day’s last rays of sun as we scarfed down a chimichurri steak burrito, two Caribbean carnitas tacos, and some garlic shrimp skewers. Hankering: Obliterated.
properties.name: Luchador Tequila & Taco Bar

properties.address: 1048 N Doctor M.L.K. Jr Dr, Milwaukee, WI 53203, USA
properties.blurb: Lucky Clover brings a modern twist to the classic Irish bar with great food and a fun atmosphere. Enjoy sharable appetizers, delectable sandwiches, and pub-classic pizzas while catching up with friends or before a local game. Pair it with a craft beer, whiskey, or bourbon, and between the lively atmosphere, hearty bites, and carefully poured drinks, it’s easy to feel like you’ve struck gold just walking through the door.
properties.name: Lucky Clover Irish Pub

properties.blurb: * Deal good on all Happy Hour Beers *
properties.name: Lucky Pie Pizza & Taphouse

properties.address: 531 S St Vrain Ave, Estes Park, CO 80517
properties.blurb: We’ve got nothing but great things to say about this little brewery. The view? Stunning. The bartenders? Cool as ice. The beers? Ice cold. Did we mention the view? Lumpy Ridge serves some of Colorado’s best craft brews in a converted gas station, giving it a rustic vibe. Take your IPA out to the patio and marvel at the view of the Rockies. Wax poetic with the locals about the hikes you’ve taken — or pretend to have taken... we don’t judge.
properties.name: Lumpy Ridge Brewing Co

properties.address: 570 Humboldt Drive, Suite 107, Big Lake, MN 55309
properties.blurb: Work Sucks. Drink Beer. That's the motto at Lupulin. And with beers like this? Maybe they're onto something. We adore the business in front, party in back attitude of Fashion Mullet. Hooey is another juicy, hazy phenom here. Not as into hops? That's cool, man. Try CPB — a Chocolate Peanut Butter Porter that's as smooth as it sounds.
properties.name: Lupulin Brewing

properties.address: 12933 Village Blvd, Madeira Beach, FL 33708, USA
properties.blurb: Mad Pub is pure madness but in the best way possible. With 63 taps flowing, over 100 craft beers to choose from, and a full liquor bar, the options are as endless as the good times. Game days? Covered, with official Bucs watch parties and plenty of screens to catch every play. Add in weekly specials, beers of the month, and the beachside buzz of John’s Pass, and you’ve got the kind of madness you’ll definitely want more of.
properties.name: Mad Pub

properties.blurb: Every day is St. Patrick's Day at Magee's Irish Pub & Eatery, where the good times flow as freely as the Guinness! Whether you're in the mood for a traditional pint of the black stuff or a unique cocktail like A Lassie's Kiss, this lively pub has you covered. And fear not, green attire is not mandatory, but it certainly adds to the festive spirit. As you raise your glass, don't forget to indulge in their mouthwatering Reuben Egg Rolls, a delightful twist on a classic Irish dish. So come on down to Magee's, where the craic is mighty and the drinks are always flowing.
properties.name: Magee's Irish Pub & Eatery

properties.blurb: Magic 13 Brewing are the masters of crafting in-house beers that'll leave you spellbound. They serve up not just a full beer menu but a mouthwatering food menu too, ensuring your taste buds are in for a real treat. And here's the kicker: they're the beer and food pairing gurus! Just ask nicely, and they'll gladly recommend the perfect beer to go with your meal, creating a symphony of flavors that'll have you savoring every bite and sip.
properties.name: Magic 13 Brewing Co

properties.address: 5841 W Bluemound Rd, Milwaukee, WI 53213, USA
properties.blurb: This is the ultimate sports bar that knows how to hit a home run when it comes to flavor. If you're a craft beer enthusiast and a sports fan, this is your new happy place, trust us, you’ll be here all the time. Imagine indulging in stadium grub that's so delicious and elevated, you'll feel like you're sitting right at the ballpark. And speaking of beer, they've got a top-notch selection of craft brews that'll quench your thirst while you cheer for your favorite team. No need to worry about missing a single play because TV's are scattered everywhere, making sure you catch all the action. And hey, if you're waiting for the next inning to start, why not challenge your buddies to a game of pool or darts?
properties.name: Magoo's on the Mound

properties.blurb: Looking for a great time with friends, high-quality beer, and live music? Main Stage Brewing has got you covered. With an awesome patio and an emphasis on creating an unforgettable experience, you won't want to miss out on their beers and delicious hand-tossed pizzas. But that's not all - Main Stage also partners with local breweries to bring you a diverse range of brews to choose from. Whether you're looking to relax with a cold beer or rock out to some great tunes, Main Stage Brewing is the perfect spot to be.
properties.name: MainStage Brewing Co

properties.blurb: At this lowkey local hangout, the bartenders are going to treat you like family. And we mean family in the good way, not like your kooky aunt with hot pink lipstick who tries to kiss your cheek at the family reunion. Nah, the folks at Malloy’s are going to welcome you with a hearty hello, a cold beer, and some insanely good food. Okay, so maybe it is a little bit like a family reunion, especially when you sneak out onto the patio to escape the kooky aunt and find your cool cousins tossing back pints.
properties.name: Malloy's Bar and Grille

properties.blurb: You’ll find the cozy sports bar Malone’s on Brady on, yup, Brady Street. If only every bar made it this easy. Grab a citrusy Hazy Rabbit IPA from Lakefront Brewery and belly up to a carpenter’s dream of a bar. When the weather is friendly, the adjoining patio bills itself as “Milwaukee’s smallest bar.” Malone’s has nearly 18 taps, bottle options galore, and a neighborhoody vibe that quickly sees first timers become regulars.
properties.name: Malone's on Brady

properties.address: 129 Holmes St S Suite 100, Shakopee, MN 55379, USA
properties.blurb: At Mana Brewing, beer and pizza are a love story as timeless as Romeo and Juliet… minus the tragic ending. With rotating seasonal beers and pizza options, you’ll be crafting your own iconic pairings faster than you can say “star-crossed lovers.” And unlike Romeo and Juliet, this tale ends with full bellies and happy hearts. Whether you stick to the classics or venture into something new, Mana’s got your perfect match—no balcony scenes required.
properties.name: Mana Brewing

properties.address: 960 E Forsyth St, Jacksonville, FL 32202, USA
properties.blurb: Allow us to introduce you to the magic of Manifest Distilling—the one and only full-scale organic distillery in Downtown Jacksonville's Sports District. These guys are on a mission to revive the spirit of American craftsmanship, and boy, do they deliver! Get ready for a journey into excellence, as Manifest takes only the finest hearts of each distillation, ensuring their spirits are as pure as it gets. Their dedication to tradition and constant exploration of new innovations sets them apart in the craft alcohol landscape. And oh, the selection! From the smooth non-GMO Potato Vodka and zesty Florida Citrus Vodka to the bold organic Gins, and the distinctive organic Rye and 100% Rye Whiskeys—they've got your taste buds covered.
properties.name: Manifest Distilling

properties.blurb: Once upon a time, Key City was home to nearly 12 breweries. Until Minnesotan Andrew Volstead, the Father of Prohibition, came along and pretty much ruined everything. Fortunately, Prohibition was repealed some 13 years later, restoring normalcy to a parched populace. In 2012, Mankato Brewery opened its doors and revived Key City’s once rich brewing tradition with beers like Kato Lager. And, well, the rest is history. Thanks for coming to our TED Talk.
properties.name: Mankato Brewery

properties.address: 1901 Delachaise St, New Orleans, LA 70115, USA
properties.name: Marsh Hotel

properties.blurb: A relative new addition to the litany of great Colorado breweries, Mash Lab is a gem of Windsor. Started by hot rod enthusiasts in a space next to a garage, Mash Lab’s vibrant and amped brews would actually be quite in sync with a flame-emblazoned 1950 Mercury Hardtop. We couldn’t say no to a Dragonfruit and Kumquat Sour, — just as delicious and tart as you might expect. Cap it off with a change of pace and order the Tennessee Tune-up Cream Ale... brewed with real Madagascar vanilla beans!
properties.name: Mash Lab Brewing & Kitchen

properties.address: 429 E Pikes Peak Ave, Colorado Springs, CO 80903
properties.blurb: Buckle up, baby. Mash Mechanix may be new on the scene, but they’re ready and raring to go. Bring your thirst for adventure and try the White Knuckle Stout or the Victory Lap Vienna Lager. Beer not your thing? They have a create-your-own hard seltzer option where patrons can mix and match flavors to dial in the perfect combo.
properties.name: Mash Mechanix Brewing Co

properties.blurb: We’re a sucker for drinks in mason jars, we can’t help ourselves. Pair this with a mouthwatering menu and we lose our minds. Add specials every night? You won’t be able to get rid of us. We started with the Garlic Cheese fries and a Quirk Strawberry Lemon Basil beer on their patio before moving inside to the warm and cozy bar. With a new burger of the month EVERY month you’ll be happy this deal refreshes every 30 days so you can come back and get next month's burger. We ended the night with a Boulevard Irish Ale and a slice of their Peanut Butter Chocolate Cheesecake and when I say it was the best cheesecake around, I really mean it. We joke a lot, but we don’t joke about cheesecake.
properties.name: Mason Jar Brews and Burgers

properties.address: 7701 Blind Pass Rd, St Pete Beach, FL 33706
properties.blurb: Imagine taking your favorite beach beer and giving it a crafty twist. That's exactly what Mastry’s Brewing nailed as the first craft brewery in sunny St. Pete Beach. This laid-back paradise serves up top-tier brews just steps from the ocean, with a food truck on site and plenty of space to kick back inside or out. Start with their crisp Breezy Blonde Ale, then dive into the seasonal lineup. And now, with their own house-made craft spirits in the mix, Mastry’s gives you even more to raise a glass to.
properties.name: Mastry's Brewing Co

properties.blurb: There’s something endearing about a husband/wife team opening a brewery together. Especially when said husband and wife offer 4 cents to local non-profits for every can of beer that they sell. Maxline Brewing also has a seriously tempting rotating food truck schedule, an outdoor stage with regular music acts, Bingo Mondays, and a variety of quality Flagship and Rotating Seasonal ales.
properties.name: Maxline Brewing

properties.blurb: Chances are, if you bump into anyone in Milwaukee, they have a story about McGillycuddy’s. After all, a bar doesn’t stick around for over 20 years for no reason. Since 2012, Milwaukee’s only official Blackhawks bar has only improved, with an expanded beer garden, a fire pit, and dining room renovations. It’s the perfect watering hole for any Milwaukee-an, Irish or not. Try the tenders. Thank us later.
properties.name: McGillycuddy's

properties.blurb: McGinns is everything you want in a neighborhood sports bar. It’s also the undisputed best pre-game spot for Brewers fans. Drop in, get yourself an order of Reuben Rolls or Deep Fried Pickles (or both, honestly, we’re not here to judge), an ice cold Miller Light (or something fancy, once again, judgment free zone), and settle in for an easy night of drinking and good company. If you happen to swing by on a Friday, well you’re in luck. Nothing better than an old fashioned Friday Fish Fry in Wisconsin.
properties.name: McGinns

properties.blurb: We wish there was a Mac’s on our block. Luckily, it’s not hard to find this gem of a neighborhood haunt. It’s also not hard to find something tasty to pair with their draft list. The pretzel sticks are apparently based on a secret 200-year-old recipe. Which makes sense since our pal Brian ate about 200 on his own. As for the rest of us? We went with brisket sandwiches and a few of Mac’s kick-ass burgers while Brian sat there lost in a pretzel haze.
properties.name: Mcintyre's Pub

properties.blurb: If you’re not paying attention, McKiernan’s might be the kind of quiet neighborhood pub you overlook. But you, our dear PubPassers, know better than that. Behind the quaint exterior is a classic Irish pub with a friendly bartender, cheap drinks, and a nice Friday night fish fry. Unleash your inner Irishman for Whiskey & Wing Wednesdays. You’re gonna love pairing a pint with the Irish nachos, too. We just know it.
properties.name: McKiernan's Irish Pub & Grill

properties.blurb: McKiernan’s does cozy pub vibes so well, you might start thinking you’re on the Emerald Isle. And if the luck of the Irish is with you, you’ve come on a Friday for the Fish Fry. Or you’ve made it on a Wednesday for Whiskey & Wing night. Or it’s a Tuesday, and you order a corned beef sandwich so delicious you start booking another trip to Ireland…before you remember you’re just a few blocks from home.
properties.name: McKiernan's Irish Tavern

properties.address: 452 N Washington Ave, Loveland, CO 80537, USA
properties.blurb: At MeadKrieger, they’re brewing Norse-inspired mead with a touch of magic right here in Colorado! Using the finest local honey, their fermentation process is pure artistry, carefully monitored to ensure every drop is exceptional. New to mead? Fear not, brave adventurer! The friendly staff are ready to guide you on your journey. Plus, they serve some tasty fœða (that's Norse for "food") that pairs perfectly with their meads and wines.
properties.name: MeadKrieger Meadery

properties.blurb: Come for the beer, discover the food, stay for the vibe. This vibrant brewpub is everything you're looking for- good tunes, fun staff, and a cozy, chill environment. They are thrilled to bring something new and exciting to this up and coming neighborhood just outside of Denver. Their traditional style ales and lagers paired with Latin American street food will have you wondering why a taqueria brewery isn't found on every corner.
properties.name: Mestizo Brew Cantina

properties.address: 1213 N Circle Dr, Colorado Springs, CO 80909
properties.blurb: No fancy conversions needed—a pint is still a pint, but it might just taste a little better on the Metric system. That’s because Metric Brewing always has something creative on tap, with new beers released weekly. Recent favorites include Blush, a strawberry-infused Belgian Blonde perfect for warmer temps, and the Colin Haze IPA. As if the beer weren’t enough, there’s a mix of different food trucks every week too.
properties.name: Metric Brewing

properties.address: 640 Rhode Island Ave NE, Washington, DC 20002
properties.blurb: What do the Washington Metro System and The Little Mermaid have in common? They’re both under DC!! Okay, we’re sorry, but we just HAD to. Anywho, moving right on…Our friends at Metrobar truly have created an amazing space here in DC. We’ve all seen and done plenty inside metro cars, it seems only fitting that we should now be able to enjoy some high class drinks and art while inside one. While you’re perusing the art displays, be sure to be sipping on some of the best craft beer brewed right in DC. What we’re saying is, we’ll see ya on down the line.
properties.name: Metrobar

properties.address: 385 Broadway St, St Paul, MN 55101, USA
properties.blurb: MetroNOME Brewery marches to the beat of its own drum, embodying the belief that education, especially in the harmonious world of music, unlocks the key to a richer and more melodious existence. This vivacious establishment hits all the right notes by dedicating a portion of their proceeds to music education, ensuring that every aspiring musician has a chance to play their tune. Prepare to be serenaded with live music performances, creating an unforgettable symphony of sounds at MetroNOME. Just like a well-conducted orchestra, their beverage selection dances to a different rhythm, with a symphony of suds, sours, and seltzers that will make your taste buds sing in perfect harmony. So, grab a pint, tap your feet to the rhythm, and join the symphonic celebration at MetroNOME Brewery.
properties.name: MetroNOME Brewery

properties.blurb: At MIA Beer Co, there are countless reasons to swing by and soak up the good times. They've got it all – from fantastic beers to crafty cocktails, top-notch spirits, and even a selection of fine wines. But if you're anything like us, you're here for one thing: the beer. And let us tell you, MIA Beer Co knows how to keep the brews flowing. Whether you're into light, hoppy, dark, amber, or strong beers, they've got you covered. It's like a flavor expedition waiting to be explored in every glass.
properties.name: MIA Beer Co

properties.address: 1811 Royal Palm Ave, Fort Myers, FL 33901
properties.blurb: Forget avocado toast—the thing millennials will be remembered for is taking the craft beer world by storm. For anyone still convinced Gen Y doesn’t like to work hard, we present Millennial Brewing Company. Original craft beers, a rotating array of food trucks, and frequent events like car shows and 5Ks are basically the definition of grind. Speaking of multitasking, is it possible to sip a pint of Namaste while in downward dog? Only one way to find out.
properties.name: Millennial Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Say their name with reverence: Millstream is Iowa’s oldest operational brewery! And their seniority shows: they’ve won 33 national awards and 1 international award since their inception. But if a glass of award-winning heritage doesn’t seal the deal for you, they also make their own root beer, cream soda, and ginger beer. So your kids can savor the atmosphere and home-brewed taste right alongside you.
properties.name: Millstream Brewing Co

properties.address: 510 Atlantic Ave, Grove City, MN 56243, USA
properties.name: Minnesota Beer Company

properties.blurb: We’re total suckers for a brewery with fun beer names, and Mirror Image delivers with ales like Kirby was a Blonde Dog and Balefire Red. Their rotating selection means there's always something new on tap, so your taste buds will never get bored. And let’s talk about the food—it's a feast! We highly recommend the Lasagna pizza, with marinara, ricotta, mozzarella, meatballs, and fresh basil. Come hungry, and leave happy!
properties.name: Mirror Image Brewing Co

properties.address: 8830 Rehco Rd, San Diego, CA 92121, USA
properties.name: Mission Brewing (Miramar)

properties.blurb: With a spacious beer garden offering views of the Mighty Mississippi, this spot gets high marks for tasty pub grub and a solid beer lineup. And yeah, most people look forward to going out with buddies on a Friday after work, but can we start a thing where we go out on Mondays instead? Like a work week kickoff happy hour? Because we’re kind of into the Monday night brew burger special. Just saying, Mondays might be the new Fridays.
